{"id":"drift-watch-qyi","title":"E1: Project Scaffold","description":"## Goal
Set up the TypeScript project skeleton with build system, test framework, CLI entry point, and npm publish readiness.

## Deliverables
- package.json with bin entry (drift-watch), type: module, tsup build, vitest test
- tsconfig.json (strict, ESM, Node 20+)
- tsup.config.ts (ESM bundle, entry: src/cli.ts)
- vitest.config.ts
- src/cli.ts: commander-based CLI with placeholder subcommands (init, scan, report, suggest, cron, status)
- .gitignore, .eslintrc or eslint.config.mjs
- npm prepublishOnly: build

## Acceptance Criteria
- pnpm build produces dist/cli.js that runs via node
- pnpm test runs vitest (even if no tests yet)
- drift-watch --help shows all 6 command groups
- drift-watch --version shows package version
- Package can be installed globally via npm link

## Tech Stack
TypeScript, tsup, commander, vitest, zod (runtime validation)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"47887658+Nathandela@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T09:57:53Z","created_by":"Nathan Delacrétaz","updated_at":"2026-03-07T09:57:53Z"}

{"id":"drift-watch-561","title":"E2: Conversation Readers","description":"## Goal
Parse conversation logs from Claude Code, Codex CLI, and Gemini CLI into a normalized format.

## Deliverables
- src/readers/types.ts: NormalizedConversation type (source, model, project, sessionId, sessionDate, messages[], toolUses[])
- src/readers/claude.ts: Parse ~/.claude/projects/*/*.jsonl
- src/readers/codex.ts: Parse ~/.codex/sessions/**/*.jsonl + metadata from ~/.codex/state_5.sqlite
- src/readers/gemini.ts: Parse ~/.gemini/tmp/*/chats/*.json
- src/readers/index.ts: discoverConversations(since?: Date) returning all conversations across sources
- Cursor-based filtering: only return conversations with file mtime > since parameter

## Acceptance Criteria
- Each reader has unit tests with fixture JSONL/JSON files
- discoverConversations() returns NormalizedConversation[] from all 3 sources
- Gracefully handles missing source directories (skip + warn)
- Gracefully handles malformed JSONL lines (skip + warn, do not crash)
- Extracts model name, project path, and session date from each source

## Data Format Reference
- Claude Code: ~/.claude/projects/<path>/<session>.jsonl - each line has type (user/assistant), message.model, message.role, message.content, timestamp, cwd, gitBranch
- Codex: ~/.codex/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-*.jsonl - session_meta (id, cwd, model_provider, cli_version), response_item (type, role, content[type,text])
- Gemini: ~/.gemini/tmp/<hash>/chats/session-*.json - sessionId, projectHash, startTime, messages[id,timestamp,type,content]","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"47887658+Nathandela@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T10:04:12Z","created_by":"Nathan Delacrétaz","updated_at":"2026-03-07T10:04:12Z"}

{"id":"drift-watch-38w","title":"E3: Dolt Storage Layer","description":"## Goal
Dolt database lifecycle management: server start/stop, schema creation, and CRUD operations for all tables.

## Deliverables
- src/storage/dolt.ts: DoltServer class with start(), stop(), isRunning(), ensureRunning(), connect() methods
  - Port derivation: FNV-32a hash of ~/.drift-watch/, range 14307-15306
  - PID file tracking at ~/.drift-watch/dolt-server.pid
  - Auto-start on first use
- src/storage/schema.sql: CREATE TABLE statements for scans, findings, patterns, finding_patterns, suggestions
- src/storage/migrations.ts: Schema versioning (version table, apply migrations on connect)
- src/storage/repository.ts: Repository class with CRUD methods for each table
- src/commands/init.ts: drift-watch init command that creates ~/.drift-watch/, initializes Dolt, runs schema, verifies dolt + claude CLIs
- src/commands/status.ts: drift-watch status command showing DB stats, last scan, server status

## Acceptance Criteria
- drift-watch init creates ~/.drift-watch/dolt/ and all schema tables
- drift-watch init checks dolt --version and claude --version, errors if missing
- drift-watch status shows table row counts and last scan timestamp
- DoltServer starts/stops Dolt process, survives parent exit (detached)
- Repository can insert/query/update all 5 tables
- Unit tests for repository CRUD (using test database)
- Integration test for init + status cycle

## Dependencies
- mysql2 npm package for MySQL protocol connection to Dolt
- ulid for ID generation","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"47887658+Nathandela@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T10:08:08Z","created_by":"Nathan Delacrétaz","updated_at":"2026-03-07T10:08:08Z"}

{"id":"drift-watch-23g","title":"E4: Analysis Engine","description":"## Goal
Headless Claude integration: spawn claude --print with structured JSON output, system prompt for drift analysis, and response parsing.

## Deliverables
- src/analysis/runner.ts: ClaudeRunner class
  - spawn() method: claude --print --output-format json --json-schema <schema> --model <model> --no-session-persistence --permission-mode bypassPermissions --system-prompt <prompt>
  - Pipe conversation data via stdin
  - Parse stdout as JSON, validate with zod
  - Handle errors (process exit codes, invalid JSON, timeouts)
- src/analysis/schema.ts: Zod schemas for Finding[] output from Claude, matching the findings table structure
- src/analysis/prompt.ts: System prompt that instructs Claude to:
  - Spawn explorer subagents to read conversation files
  - Detect: repeated mistakes, ignored instructions, over-engineering, security issues, anti-patterns, hallucinated APIs, inefficient tool usage
  - Extract: source, model, project, session date, severity, evidence excerpts, tool usage context
  - Output structured JSON matching the schema
- src/analysis/index.ts: analyze(conversations: NormalizedConversation[]) -> Finding[]

## Acceptance Criteria
- ClaudeRunner spawns claude process and captures JSON output
- Output validates against zod schema
- System prompt produces meaningful findings from a sample conversation
- Handles claude not found (clear error)
- Handles timeout (configurable, default 5 minutes per batch)
- Handles invalid JSON response (retry once, then error)
- Unit tests with mocked claude process
- Integration test with real claude --print (manual, not CI)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"47887658+Nathandela@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T10:32:33Z","created_by":"Nathan Delacrétaz","updated_at":"2026-03-07T10:32:33Z"}

{"id":"drift-watch-36n","title":"E5: Scan Pipeline","description":"## Goal
Wire readers, analysis engine, and storage into the complete scan pipeline with cursor tracking and pattern aggregation.

## Deliverables
- src/commands/scan.ts: drift-watch scan command that:
  1. Reads last scan cursors from scans table
  2. Calls discoverConversations(since) to get new conversations
  3. Groups conversations by project for batched analysis
  4. Calls analyze() per batch via headless Claude
  5. Stores findings in Dolt
  6. Matches findings to existing patterns or creates new ones
  7. Updates scan record with new cursors
  8. Creates Dolt commit
- src/storage/patterns.ts: Pattern matching logic
  - Match findings to patterns by category + title similarity
  - Create new patterns for unmatched findings
  - Update occurrence_count, last_seen on matched patterns
- Cursor persistence: store last-processed mtime per source in scans table

## Acceptance Criteria
- drift-watch scan reads conversations, analyzes, stores findings, commits Dolt
- Second scan only processes new conversations (cursor works)
- Findings are linked to patterns via finding_patterns junction table
- New patterns created when no match found
- Existing patterns updated when match found (occurrence_count incremented)
- Scan creates a Dolt commit with descriptive message
- Handles empty scan gracefully (no new conversations = no-op)
- Integration test for full scan pipeline (with mocked Claude runner)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"47887658+Nathandela@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T10:33:53Z","created_by":"Nathan Delacrétaz","updated_at":"2026-03-07T10:33:53Z"}

{"id":"drift-watch-2aq","title":"E6: Reporting","description":"## Goal
Query and display aggregated drift patterns from Dolt with filtering options.

## Deliverables
- src/commands/report.ts: drift-watch report command with flags:
  - (default): Display patterns sorted by occurrence_count descending
  - --by-model: Group findings by AI model
  - --by-project: Group findings by project path
  - --since <date>: Filter findings after date
  - --category <cat>: Filter by finding category
  - --limit <n>: Limit results (default 20)
- src/display/table.ts: Terminal table formatting (using built-in console.table or simple column alignment)
- src/display/summary.ts: Summary statistics (total findings, top patterns, most affected projects)

## Acceptance Criteria
- drift-watch report shows patterns with title, category, occurrence count, severity, first/last seen
- --by-model groups findings and shows per-model counts
- --by-project groups findings and shows per-project counts
- --since filters correctly
- Handles empty database gracefully (no findings = helpful message)
- Unit tests for query building and display formatting","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"47887658+Nathandela@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T10:34:36Z","created_by":"Nathan Delacrétaz","updated_at":"2026-03-07T10:34:36Z"}

{"id":"drift-watch-550","title":"E7: Suggestions Engine","description":"## Goal
Generate corrective strategy suggestions by feeding top patterns to headless Claude.

## Deliverables
- src/commands/suggest.ts: drift-watch suggest command that:
  1. Queries top patterns from Dolt (by occurrence count and severity)
  2. Feeds pattern descriptions + example findings to headless Claude
  3. Requests concrete corrective strategies categorized as: claude_md_patch, linter_rule, test_case, documentation, user_training
  4. Stores suggestions in the suggestions table
  5. Displays suggestions to user
- src/analysis/suggest-prompt.ts: System prompt for strategy generation
  - Instruct Claude to produce actionable, concrete suggestions
  - Include artifact field for copy-pasteable snippets (CLAUDE.md additions, test stubs, linter configs)
- src/analysis/suggest-schema.ts: Zod schema for Suggestion[] output

## Acceptance Criteria
- drift-watch suggest shows corrective strategies for top patterns
- Each suggestion has a strategy_type, title, description, and optional artifact
- Suggestions are stored in Dolt for future reference
- --pattern <id> flag to suggest for a specific pattern
- --limit <n> flag to control how many patterns to process (default 5)
- Handles no patterns gracefully
- Unit tests with mocked Claude output","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"47887658+Nathandela@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T10:35:19Z","created_by":"Nathan Delacrétaz","updated_at":"2026-03-07T10:35:19Z"}

{"id":"drift-watch-h5q","title":"E8: Cron, Config, and Publish","description":"## Goal
Cron job management for periodic scans, configuration file support, and final polish for npm publish.

## Deliverables
- src/commands/cron.ts: drift-watch cron subcommands:
  - install [--interval <cron-expr>]: Add crontab entry for drift-watch scan (default: weekly Sunday 3am)
  - remove: Remove the crontab entry
  - status: Show whether cron is installed and last run
- src/config/index.ts: Config management
  - Read/write ~/.drift-watch/config.json
  - Defaults: scan_interval, claude_model (sonnet), categories (all), excluded_projects ([]), dolt_port
  - drift-watch config show: Display current config
  - drift-watch config set <key> <value>: Update config
- Cron log output to ~/.drift-watch/logs/<date>.log
- npm publish readiness: README with install/usage instructions, LICENSE

## Acceptance Criteria
- drift-watch cron install adds entry to crontab -l
- drift-watch cron remove cleans it up
- drift-watch cron status shows current cron state
- Config file is created on init with defaults
- Config values are respected by scan, report, suggest commands
- Cron-invoked scan logs to ~/.drift-watch/logs/
- Package passes npm pack without errors","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","owner":"47887658+Nathandela@users.noreply.github.com","created_at":"2026-03-07T10:40:13Z","created_by":"Nathan Delacrétaz","updated_at":"2026-03-07T10:40:13Z"}
